content.problemStatement:
A programming competition will be held in Chef's city which only accepts teams of size 222. At least one person in a team should know a programming language and at least one person should know about the English language. It is possible that one person in a team knows both programming and the English language. Then the qualification of the other teammate is not important i.e. he may or may not know programming and English. Now Chef has NNN students and wants to send maximum number of teams to the competition.

You are given an integer NNN and two binary strings SSS and TTT of length NNN. Si=S_i = Si​= `'1'` denotes that the ithi^{th}ith student knows a programming language, and Si=S_i = Si​= `'0'` denotes otherwise; similarly, Ti=T_i =Ti​= `'1'` denotes that the ithi^{th}ith student knows English language, and Ti=T_i = Ti​= `'0'` denotes otherwise. Determine the maximum number of teams Chef can form.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer QQQ denoting the number of test cases. The description of QQQ test cases follows.
Each test case contains 333 lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains a string SSS.
The last line of each test case contains a string TTT.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the maximum number of teams that Chef can form.
constraints: S and T consist only of characters '0' and '1'
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer Q, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer N, the number of students. The second line contains a binary string S of length N, where S_i = '1' means the i-th student knows programming. The third line contains a binary string T of length N, where T_i = '1' means the i-th student knows English.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with one integer – the maximum number of valid teams of size 2 that can be formed.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: Students are (P only), (none), (both). Pair the third student (both) with any other → 1 team.
Test case 2: Students: (P only), (both), (P only), (both). Form two teams: (1st,2nd) and (3rd,4th).
Test case 3: No programmer at all, so 0 teams.
Test case 4: One student knows both, the other knows only English, they form a valid team.
Counts: A=2, B=2, C=1, D=0. Pair the two A‑B pairs → 2 teams. The remaining C cannot form another team alone.
All students know neither skill, so no valid team can be created.

solution.approach: Classify each student into one of four types:  • A: knows only programming (S=1, T=0)  • B: knows only English (S=0, T=1)  • C: knows both (S=1, T=1)  • D: knows none (S=0, T=0)  A valid team must contain at least one programmer and at least one English speaker. The only ways to satisfy this are:  • Pair an A with a B  • Pair a C with anyone (including another C or a D)  The optimal strategy is:  1. Pair as many A‑B pairs as possible – this uses two non‑C students per team and is always optimal.  2. After step 1, the remaining students are some A’s, some B’s, some D’s and the C’s. Each remaining A, each remaining B and each D needs a C to form a valid team. Let demand = remaining_A + remaining_B + D. Use min(C, demand) C‑students to satisfy this demand – each such use creates one team.  3. Any C‑students left after step 2 can only be paired among themselves, giving floor(remaining_C / 2) additional teams.  The total number of teams is:  AB + usedC + floor((C‑usedC)/2) where AB = min(A, B) and usedC = min(C, demand). The algorithm runs in O(N) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    Q = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(Q):
        N = int(next(it))
        S = next(it).strip()
        T = next(it).strip()
        a=b=c=d=0
        for s,t in zip(S,T):
            if s=='1' and t=='0':
                a+=1
            elif s=='0' and t=='1':
                b+=1
            elif s=='1' and t=='1':
                c+=1
            else:
                d+=1
        AB = min(a,b)
        a -= AB
        b -= AB
        demand = a + b + d
        usedC = min(c, demand)
        remainingC = c - usedC
        ans = AB + usedC + remainingC//2
        out_lines.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
